original_hypothesis.title: Multicall.getRevertMessage uses non-standard memory manipulation that may mis-decode revert data and obscure failure handling
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Multicall.getRevertMessage attempts to decode revert strings by manually padding and modifying bytes offsets (setting paddedData[63]=0x40). This is unusual and may mis-handle certain revert payloads (custom errors, panic codes, short data), potentially causing multicall to revert with 'MC' and obscure underlying errors. While not a direct fund-loss vector, it can impede operational debugging and, in edge cases, cause unexpected reverts if decoding logic itself fails under specific calldata patterns.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Given multicall is a core execution primitive used by MarginTradingHook and MoneyMarketHook, robustness of revert propagation matters. Incorrect decoding could hinder monitoring/automation and complicate incident response; also could be used to force generic reverts ('MC') to hide exploit traces in bundled calls.

proof_of_signal.pattern: manual revert-string decoding via abi.encodePacked padding and direct byte offset mutation (paddedData[63]=0x40)

preconditions: A delegatecall in multicall fails and returns revert data in a format not compatible with the decoding approach (e.g., custom error, panic, ABI-encoded string with different offset expectations)
System relies on accurate revert bubbling for correct off-chain decision-making

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: nonstandard revert-data decoding that may fail/misdecode
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: may mask real revert reasons or behave inconsistently across EVM implementations
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No explicit handling for Error(string) selector vs Panic(uint256) vs custom errors

analysis_questions: Does getRevertMessage correctly decode standard `Error(string)` payloads for all lengths?
What happens for Panic(uint256) and custom errors—does it revert, return garbage, or default to 'MC'?
Can an attacker craft revert data to cause unexpected behavior or excessive gas usage in decoding?
validation_requirements: Property-based tests generating revert payloads (Error/Panic/custom/short) and verifying behavior
Gas profiling of failure paths for large revert payloads
Compare against OpenZeppelin Multicall revert bubbling patterns

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Attacker causes a multicall subcall to revert with crafted payload so that the system reports only 'MC', obscuring root cause; if any off-chain automation depends on revert reasons, it may make unsafe assumptions or fail to remediate.
prerequisites: Ability to trigger failing subcalls inside multicall (generally possible by input selection)

potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users executing via multicall-enabled hooks
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Upgrade Multicall to robust revert bubbling implementation

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Likely no tests for revert bubbling across different revert formats

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
